creator: ade@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, May 22, 2000 at 03:33:51PM -0700, David O'Brien wrote:
> If devel/gettext is a dependacy for Mutt, then that is something else
> that I have to keep updated, else I cannot maintain mutt.  Since so many
> other things (such as Gtk and things that depend on it) are also
> dependant on it, I could break other binaries in updating my installation
> of gettext just to compile Mutt.  I am not willing to go that path.

From rev 1.86 of ports/mail/mutt/Makefile, line 34:

BUILD_DEPENDS=	msgfmt:${PORTSDIR}/devel/gettext

so it's already a dependency.  It was last version-updated in 1998
(everything since then has been tweaks, such as making shared
libraries, installing things in 'proper' places).  ie: it's stable.

As for the rest of your argument, I take it then that you keep an
eye on devel/libslang, textproc/ispell, textproc/sgmlformat,
devel/ncurses, openssl, textproc/urlview and all the ports that
depend on those, just to keep mutt safe and sound.

Twice you have brought up the GTK strawman..  it's irrelevant in
this case.

-aDe

-- 
Ade Lovett, Austin, TX.
